London, United Kingdom

ProLuxe Bureau

Rooms next to the principal, not down the road.

Bureau is the console we built for our office. It holds the joint engagement, principal and protection team, in a single record. Hotel relationships at director level, one point of contact across travel and security, the same record the hotel works from.

01 / Same property as the principal.

Same property as the principal.

Hotel relationships at director level make placement on the same floor or adjacent rooms possible. Not central reservations, not a junior duty manager. The conversation that allows it happens before the team arrives.

02 / Op centre when the property allows.

Op centre when the property allows.

Coordinated through the GM rather than pieced together by the team on arrival. Function space, secondary suites, service routes, agreed in advance and held against the engagement.

03 / One contact across travel and security.

One contact across travel and security.

The same record we work from, the same record the hotel sees, the same person to call when something moves. Travel logistics and protection coverage in one view, not two parallel inboxes.

How it works for operations

We configure Bureau before the engagement opens. Principal accommodation, protection team rooms, any op centre arrangement, all sitting on one record against one hotel relationship. From there, both halves of the trip stay in step.

If a movement changes, you tell us once. The principal record and the team record update together. The hotel works from the same file. Nothing goes through 2 inboxes to find the same answer.

bureau.proluxetravels.com/engagements/[engagement]/joint

Op centre arranged via the GM. Boardroom B, service entrance, held.

When the placement matters

Most engagements never test the room next to the principal. The ones that do tend to fall in a few predictable windows.

Pre-arrival checks 2 to 3 days out, with the property already briefed on who is staying and where. Room inspection on the day of arrival, with the GM on the same call rather than escalated to. A last-minute move at 6pm, where the existing relationship is the difference between a yes and a polite no.

Bureau holds the record. The relationship sits behind it. Both are quiet until the engagement asks for them.

Operational windows

2 to 3 days before arrival

Pre-arrival checks. The property is briefed on who is staying, room set-up confirmed against the record.

Arrival day

Room inspection with the duty GM. Same record, no re-briefing on the doorstep.

Last-minute movement

Venue swap, late move, sweep coordination. The director-level relationship is the call you do not want to make from scratch.

Always on hand

If a placement needs us, a late move, a venue swap, a sweep coordination, pass it back. We pick it up, the audit log shows the handoff, and the principal and team records stay in step.

Pass back

Late move, principal hotel change inside 24 hours of arrival.
Venue swap, dinner or meeting venue change with team accommodation knock-on.
Sweep coordination, room access window agreed with the GM before the team arrives.

If you are placing a protection team near a principal, write. Hotel relationships at director level make the difference on placement, timing, and what the property will agree to in advance.

Speak with us
